module.name: agent
module.purpose: Agent crate namespace for session runtime orchestration, model backend abstractions, and the implemented task execution runtime. lib.rs is re-export wiring and now also re-exports TaskContext; the session child module owns Session and SessionContext, the model child module owns the backend abstraction and Deepseek backend, and the task child module owns the Task runtime, shared TaskContext state, step, and execution types.

childModules.name: model
childModules.purpose: Owns agent model backend traits, request/response data, backend errors, and the implemented Deepseek streaming backend. ModelBackendImpl exposes only send, bridged to ModelBackend by a blanket impl; DeepseekBackend loads its config and issues streaming Deepseek chat-completions requests, and ModelBackendError converts io, serde_json, and reqwest errors.

childModules.name: session
childModules.purpose: Owns Session startup and SessionContext task/event routing runtime data behind the agent session namespace; Session::new spawns the client/host workers directly (no separate run) and creates tasks synchronously via Task::new, guarding its shared client/host mpsc endpoints with std::sync::Mutex and reporting CreateFailed via try_send.

childModules.name: task
childModules.purpose: Owns the implemented task runtime: Task drives model steps on a worker thread spawned directly in Task::new (no separate run), and streams task status to the client plus forwards host execution events by synchronously locking client_tx (std::sync::Mutex) and calling try_send, while the TaskContext holds shared runtime state (signature, std::sync::Mutex-guarded model backend, client/host senders, and execution and step WorkQueues). step.rs and execution.rs define the step and execution records; mod.rs is namespace and re-export wiring only.
